title: A Realtor Pulled Up to List a House. It Was Full of Snakes.
script: Who put snakes in here? Realtor Christopher White from Century 21 pulled up to a vacant house in rural central Arkansas to show it to a potential buyer. Routine showing. Except when he looked at the porch — snakes. Black rat snakes draped over the electrical conduit. Snakes trying to squeeze through the closed front door. Snakes in the old electric panel. Dozens of them, non-venomous but EVERYWHERE. White filmed the whole thing. Then he texted his buyer: I am absolutely not going to show this house. No one knows how they got in. Power's disconnected. The house sits empty. Turns out black rat snakes love abandoned buildings — they're basically free pest control for farms. So: who's making an offer on the snake house?
